$comment: D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR (SQLite testfixture arc, C1 2026-07-17): the neutral <tcl.h> FFI descriptor. SQLite's `testfixture` (the binary that runs the 1190-file .test suite = 'all sqlite units') is ~90 dense C TUs linked against libtcl8.6, and every src/test*.c uses TCL's C API. testfixture calls TCL DIRECTLY — its build recipe (main.mk `make -n testfixture`) carries NO -DUSE_TCL_STUBS (that flag is only on the loadable-EXTENSION targets tclsqlite-{shell,stubs}.o), and /usr/include/tcl8.6/tcl.h has ZERO tclStubsPtr references — so these are plain extern prototypes, NOT the stubs-table macro form. That makes tcl.json an ORDINARY (if large) FFI descriptor, exactly like the libc ones. | LINKAGE: the elf `library` is libtcl8.6.so (TCL's SONAME — it ships an UNVERSIONED soname; `readelf -d libtcl8.6.so.0` -> SONAME libtcl8.6.so, and the runtime pkg libtcl8.6 ships BOTH libtcl8.6.so and the libtcl8.6.so.0 compat file, so the soname resolves without tcl8.6-dev). The ELF walker emits one DT_NEEDED per DISTINCT referenced import library (D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED, the libm.so.6 precedent), so a <tcl.h> user links libtcl8.6.so ALONGSIDE libc.so.6 — witnessed by examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_eval (RUN -> exit 42 on elf x86_64). | availableObjectFormats:[elf] — TCL exists on pe/macho too but with different sonames + no run witness yet; a pe/macho <tcl.h> include fails loud until those legs land (later arc cycle). | OPAQUE HANDLES: Tcl_Interp/Tcl_Obj are modeled like stdio.json's FILE — a named struct with an opaque byte blob, used only via ptr<...> (never by-value), so member access fails loud and the blob size is inert. C1 shipped the eval surface (5 functions); C2 (2026-07-17) adds the COMMAND-REGISTRATION surface (Tcl_NewIntObj/Tcl_SetObjResult + Tcl_CreateObjCommand, whose 3rd param is a FUNCTION POINTER `ptr<fn(...)>` — the signal.json handler precedent) so a C command can be registered and CALLED BACK by libtcl — the core shape of every SQLite test*.c command (witnessed by examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_command → 42). 8 functions total; the full 106-symbol test-TU surface + the real Tcl_Obj layout + the CONST/Tcl_Size PP-macros land as later cycles' real test-TU consumers demand them.

symbols.name: Tcl_GetIntFromObj
symbols.signature: fn(ptr<struct "Tcl_Interp" { arr<u64, 1> }>, ptr<struct "Tcl_Obj" { arr<u64, 6> }>, ptr<i32>) -> i32

symbols.name: Tcl_CreateObjCommand
symbols.signature: fn(ptr<struct "Tcl_Interp" { arr<u64, 1> }>, ptr<char>, ptr<fn(ptr<void>, ptr<struct "Tcl_Interp" { arr<u64, 1> }>, i32, ptr<ptr<struct "Tcl_Obj" { arr<u64, 6> }>>) -> i32>, ptr<void>, ptr<void>) -> ptr<void>
